content.question: Whether a phased shift from 8-year to 6-year grammar school entry (age 12), preserving selection but delaying it, would reduce social stratification or merely postpone it is a structural question not addressed in the current evidence base.
content.reduction_path: Comparative research on countries that have experimentally implemented age-postponement of tracking (e.g., comparing 10 vs. 12 entry regimes in the same institutional context), ideally with randomized school-network pilots in Hungary.
